channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: 340 Miles, One Charging Stop, and a Lot of German Motorway. The BMW iX3 Actually Delivered.
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl drives the base BMW iX3, with the 108.7 kWh battery and 20-inch wheels, 550 km to Berlin at Autobahn speeds and back without charging. On 20-inch wheels, consumption at 100 km/h came to 16 kWh per 100 km, a real-world range of roughly 680 km. At 130 km/h, that rose to 22 kWh, good for around 500 km. The outbound charging stop at a 400 kW station went from 15% to 80% in approximately 18 minutes, averaging 280 kW. The return trip covered 530 km with 10% remaining, pointing to a maximum of about 590 km on one charge.

item.title: 40,000 Miles, One Deer, Two Tire Disasters. Car and Driver's BMW i4 Test Is Finally Done.
item.dc:creator: Car and Driver
item.description: Car and Driver put a 2024 BMW i4 xDrive40 through 40,000 miles. The xDrive40 makes 396 horsepower and tested to 60 mph in 4.4 seconds. EPA range is 279 miles on 19-inch wheels. The factory Hankook summer tires chunked during instrumented testing. An aftermarket winter tire set introduced sloppy handling that BMW-spec Goodyear Ultragrip tires fixed. A deer strike cost $16,500 in repairs. One scheduled service visit in 40,000 miles covered everything under BMW's complimentary program. Multiple editors called it one of the best-driving BMWs regardless of powertrain.

item.title: The Renault 5 and Nissan Micra Share the Same Platform, Battery, and Factory. One of Them Costs Less Per Month and Looks Better.
item.dc:creator: Electrifying
item.description: The Renault 5 and new Nissan Micra share the same platform, 52 kWh battery, 148 horsepower motor, and French factory. Both charge at 11 kW AC and 100 kW DC. The Renault 5 Techno Plus costs £23,945 after the £3,750 grant; the Micra Advance costs £23,245. On finance, the Renault works out to £348 per month versus £371 for the Micra. Driven back to back on English B-roads, both presenters found the two indistinguishable by feel. The Micra has regen paddles for one-pedal driving that the Renault 5 does not yet offer. The Renault wins on styling, finance, and interior personality.

item.title: Ethiopia Banned Gas and Diesel Car Imports and Built Africa's Biggest Dam. Its EV Fleet Has Nearly Quadrupled Since.
item.dc:creator: DW REV
item.description: Ethiopia is targeting half a million EVs on the road by 2030, with over 97 percent of its electricity from hydropower. The Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam, Africa's largest, began operation in 2025 at 5,150 megawatts. Over 100 electric buses carry more than 90,000 passengers daily in Addis Ababa. The EV fleet has grown from around 30,000 vehicles two years ago to nearly 120,000. A 2024 ban on gasoline and diesel car imports was driven by a fuel import bill of close to four billion euros annually. Around 500 charging stations exist nationally, nearly all in the capital.

item.title: Walmart Just Opened Its First Charger North of the 37th Parallel. It Costs 37% Less Than Nearby Tesla.
item.dc:creator: State of Charge
item.description: Walmart's DC fast charging network now has 31 locations, with the newest at a Freehold, New Jersey Supercenter, the first site north of the 37th parallel. The station uses eight Alpitronic HYC400 chargers rated at up to 400 kW each, with both CCS and NACS connectors per pedestal. State of Charge paid $0.39 per kWh. The nearest Tesla Supercharger about two miles away was showing $0.62 per kWh at the same time. Charging requires the Walmart app and a QR scan. Walmart is not adding new third-party chargers to its properties, and existing network agreements are expected to wind down as leases expire.

item.title: BYD's Luxury EV Has 1,156 HP, Closes Its Own Doors, and Charges in Nine Minutes
item.dc:creator: Autotrader
item.description: The Denza Z9 GT is BYD's premium performance EV at around £105,000 in the UK. Three motors produce 1,156 PS and 1,200 Nm of torque. The 122 kWh battery claims 600 km WLTP range. Using BYD's own flash chargers, it goes from 10 to 97 percent in nine minutes. Those chargers store energy in Blade Battery packs on-site, requiring only a modest grid connection. Denza plans 3,000 of them across Europe within 12 months. Rear-wheel steering enables crab walk, a pencil compass spin-on-the-spot, and a 4.6 m turning circle. Autotrader had three hours with the car on French roads.

item.title: Porsche's Wireless Charger Hits 92% Efficiency. Engineering Explained Was a Skeptic. Not Anymore.
item.dc:creator: Engineering Explained
item.description: Engineering Explained's Jason Fenske went in expecting to dismiss wireless EV charging on the Porsche Cayenne Electric and came out convinced. The system achieves 89 to 92 percent efficiency, better than Level 1 wired at 60 percent. At 11 kW it charges faster than Porsche's mobile connector. Floor pad costs around $6,500, vehicle receiver around $1,650. Over 200,000 miles the extra cost of wireless versus Level 2 wired is roughly $1,000. Safety systems include radar for living objects, metal detection, and temperature sensors. Oak Ridge National Lab tested wireless charging on a Porsche Taycan at 270 kW and 95 percent efficiency.

item.title: 431 Miles of Range, Best-in-Class Frunk, and Door Handles That Refuse to Work. The Lucid Air Is Almost Brilliant.
item.dc:creator: TheStraightPipes
item.description: Jacob from TheStraightPipes spends a week with the 2026 Lucid Air Touring: 620 hp, 885 lb-ft, 92 kWh battery, and a rated 431-mile range. In cold weather around 3°C, 50% battery showed approximately 300 km remaining. The 900V architecture supports up to 350 kW DC charging. The glass canopy and best-in-class frunk earn genuine praise. Wireless CarPlay worked until it stopped. The key fob has no buttons. Door handles sometimes do not present. Starts at $112,800 CAD.

item.title: Tesla FSD Is Live in the Netherlands. Here's What Has to Happen Before the Rest of Europe Gets It.
item.dc:creator: Autospec Roaming
item.description: The Dutch Vehicle Authority approved Tesla FSD on April 10 after 18 months of testing and 1.6 million-plus kilometres on EU roads. EU-wide rollout requires a majority vote from all 27 member states. The EU version differs from the US one: no speed profiles, on-screen notifications for every manoeuvre, stricter driver monitoring, and pre-approval required for future software updates. The system turns off when crossing into a non-approved country. Purchase price is €7,500, with a €99 monthly subscription now live in the Netherlands.

item.title: The Lightship's Trek Drive Let a Rivian R1S Tow 80 Miles on 36% Battery and Arrive With 13% Left
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Kyle loads the Lightship onto a Rivian R1S at 36% battery with 80 miles to go. Trek Drive, the trailer's 78 kWh battery and rear-axle motor, pushed 13 to 30 kW throughout the run. Efficiency in the Rivian held at 2.3 to 2.45 miles per kWh while towing at up to 70 mph. They arrived with 13% in the truck and 70% in the trailer after using only about 30 kWh from the Rivian across 71 miles. Without Trek Drive, they would not have made it. The Lightship starts around $155,000 and climbs above $170,000 with Trek Drive.

item.title: The Renault 5 Has Been the Best Small EV for Over a Year. What Car? Just Found Something Better.
item.dc:creator: What Car?
item.description: What Car? runs the Renault 5, MG4 Urban, and Hyundai Inster through two days of city driving, motorway runs, interior tests, a drag race, and real-world range calculations. The MG4 Urban long range premium is more efficient, more practical, better equipped, and the only one with a five-star Euro NCAP rating. The Renault 5 Techno Plus costs £23,945 after the government grant, pulled hardest at the fast charger, and is the most fun to drive. The Inster has the best rear headroom, sliding rear seats, and returned the highest potential real-world range at 190 miles. The Renault 5 lost its crown.

item.title: Toyota Fixed the Range, Kept the Reliability, and Added a 10-Year Warranty. The C-HR Plus Is Hard to Ignore.
item.dc:creator: CarGurus UK
item.description: The Toyota C-HR Plus mid-spec Design trim packs a 77 kWh battery, 221 horsepower, and a 377-mile WLTP range. In real-world testing, CarGurus UK saw just over 4 miles per kWh and well over 300 miles from a full charge. Rapid charging tops out at 150 kW. Entry-level Icon trim qualifies for the £1,500 electric car grant, putting the base car under £33,000. Service the car at Toyota and the warranty extends to 10 years and 100,000 miles, with the battery guaranteed to hold at least 70% capacity for 10 years. The rear seat is the honest compromise.

item.title: A Used Porsche Taycan and a New Tesla Model 3 Performance Now Cost the Same. Which One Do You Actually Want?
item.dc:creator: Gjeebs
item.description: Depreciation has done something interesting. A used Porsche Taycan 4S has landed at roughly the same price as a new Tesla Model 3 Performance. The Model 3 Performance hits 0-60 in 2.9 seconds, runs Tesla's Supercharger network with Grok-powered AI navigation, has better trunk space, superior rear passenger room, and seats Gjeebs calls some of the best ever made. The Taycan brings genuine sports car feel, air suspension, and presence. The Taycan wins on feel. The Model 3 wins on everything practical.

item.title: Tires Built Specifically for Electric Trucks Are a Real Thing Now, and This One Is Genuinely Good
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Kyle from Out of Spec Reviews puts the Hankook iON HT on his Rivian R1T and runs it on a private track and extended highway miles. The iON HT is an all-season tire engineered for the weight, torque, and rolling resistance demands of electric trucks and large SUVs. On the highway at 75 mph, the electric motors were louder than the tires, which is the compliment. After 500 to 700 miles on the R1T, efficiency has been notably strong.

item.title: Cars Have Not Done This Yet. This Motorcycle Has Solid-State as Standard.
item.dc:creator: Verge Motorcycles
item.description: Verge Motorcycles put solid-state batteries into production in Q1 2026 with the TS Pro, the first production vehicle of any kind to ship solid-state as standard. The base pack holds 20.2 kWh total with 18 kWh nominal capacity and a real-world range claim of up to 217 miles. It charges at up to 100 kW, reaching 80% in under 10 minutes. The solid-state chemistry runs air cooling only with no liquid cooling system. The hub motor produces 102 kW and 1,000 Nm of torque. Zero to 100 km/h takes 3.5 seconds.

item.title: VW Listened to Every Complaint. The New ID.3 Neo Has Real Buttons, a Real Interior, and Something to Prove.
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Thomas from Autogefühl walks through the pre-production VW ID.3 Neo. Four individual window switches replace the infamous single-button layout. Physical climate dials are back. A 10-inch instrument cluster and 13-inch infotainment screen arrive alongside a steering wheel with actual tactile buttons. The 79 kWh NMC pack hits 168 kW DC and 10 to 80 percent in around 29 minutes. New LFP options start at 50 kWh and 58 kWh. WLTP range clears 600 km, and real motorway cruising lands around 400 km. Thomas calls the update a genuine turning point for a brand that had lost its way.

item.title: The Cybertruck Blinked. The Rivian Didn't. First Lightship Trek Drive Tow Test Results Are In.
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec BITS
item.description: Kyle gets the first press loan of the Lightship aero-electric trailer alongside Cybertruck owner Maryanne and co-founder Ben. At 75 mph, the Cybertruck towed the Lightship solidly. Then a left turn signal and brake light issue with the seven-pin connector prevented Trek Drive from being tested. The Rivian R1T dual motor stepped in, Trek Drive activated immediately, held 15 to 20 kW at 65 mph, scaled to 30 kW on hills, and cut off cleanly at around 77 mph. DC fast charging on the trailer at up to 150 kW is still coming via software update.

item.title: Rivian's Old Battery Packs Are Getting a Second Life Powering the Factory That Built Them
item.dc:creator: Rivian Newsroom
item.description: New U.S. grid infrastructure takes 5 to 12 years to build. Rivian and Redwood Materials are not waiting. According to a Rivian press release, the two companies are deploying second-life Rivian battery packs at the Normal, Illinois plant as on-site peak demand storage. It is the first known closed-loop program where an automaker's retired packs power the factory that built them. Redwood was founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel. Nissan, BMW, and VW have all tried second-life battery projects. None closed the loop like this.

item.title: BYD Cut the Price, Added a Frunk, and Jumped to 800V. The Atto 3 Evo Is Hard to Argue With.
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric CARS
item.description: The BYD Atto 3 Evo arrives in the UK with a frunk the original never had, a 585-litre boot (over 1,000 with seats folded), 800V architecture matching a Porsche Taycan for charge speed, and a battery that grew from around 60 kWh to 74.8 kWh at the same weight. Every variant costs approximately 700 pounds less than the equivalent original Atto 3. UK pricing sits between 37,000 and 42,000 pounds. The 8-year, 150,000-mile battery warranty has not changed.

item.title: 64,000 Solar Panels, a Community Farm, and Some Very Unhappy Neighbours. Australia's Clean Energy Revolution Has a People Problem.
item.dc:creator: SBS News
item.description: SBS News reporter Alexandra Jones travels to Dubbo, NSW, the first Renewable Energy Zone in New South Wales, to find out who actually benefits when wind and solar farms reshape regional Australia. She visits an agrisolar farm under 64,000 panels, a community cooperative in Orange, and an Aboriginal Land Council that secured a stake in a major battery facility. Researchers say resolving First Nations land claims could unlock up to 18 times more solar and 22 times more wind in those areas. Some neighbouring landowners report falling property values.
